results.description:
AIM 1: DESIGN BIOMIMETIC COLLAGEN MICROSTRUCTURES DERIVED FROM NATIVE TRACHEAL ECM STRUCTURE THAT RECAPITULATE PHYSIOLOGIC
MECHANICAL PROPERTIES. THROUGHOUT THE TRACHEA THE MICROSTRUCTURE OF THE ECM VARIES CONSIDERABLY TO SUPPORT REGIONAL
MECHANICAL LOADING. STANDARD 3D PRINTING NEGLECTS THIS COMPLEXITY: WE MUST DEVELOP A NEW WAY TO PRINT COLLAGEN TO
RECAPITULATE THESE ESSENTIAL MECHANICAL FEATURES. WE CAN DO SO BY CHARACTERIZING AND THEN MIMICKING THE REGIONAL ECM
MICROSTRUCTURE OF PARTS OF THE TRACHEA (E.G. RINGS, CONNECTIVE TISSUE) APPROACH: AIM 1.1: HIGH RESOLUTION MICRO COMPUTED
TOMOGRAPHY (ÃƑÅ½Ã‚Â¼CT), OPTICAL COHERENCE TOMOGRAPHY (OCT), AND SECOND HARMONIC GENERATION (SHG) MICROSCOPY WILL BE
PERFORMED ON COMMERCIALLY SOURCED PORCINE TRACHEAS TO IDENTIFY FUNCTIONALLY ESSENTIAL MICROSTRUCTURAL ECM FEATURES. AIM 1.2:
DESIGN SEVERAL BIOMIMETIC MICROSTRUCTURAL PATTERNS DERIVED FROM DIFFERENT PARTS OF THE TRACHEA AND COMPARE THEIR MECHANICAL
PROPERTIES TO SIMPLE STACKED SQUARE GRID PATTERNS USING FINITE ELEMENT ANALYSIS (FEA) OF RING-SHAPED GEOMETRIES. AIM 1.3:
RINGS WITH BIOMIMETIC AND STACKED SQUARE GRID MICROSTRUCTURAL PATTERNS WILL BE PRINTED FROM COLLAGEN TYPE I FOLLOWED BY TENSILE
AND COMPLIANCE TESTING OF EACH PATTERN. THIS DATA WILL THEN BE USED TO VERIFY THE FEA MODEL.
results.recipient_name: UNIVERSITY OF PITTSBURGH - OF THE COMMONWEALTH SYSTEM OF HIGHER EDUCATION
